### Fix 3
Line: 68
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FormFieldGroupHeader

Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

```
   69                  aria-label={t('Remove peer')}

```

I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 74): `Button variant="plain"` type changed
The variant `"plain"` still exists in the new type (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. Since `"plain"` is still valid, **no code change is needed**.

### Fix 2 (Line 6): `FormFieldGroupExpandable` signature changed
The signature changed from `React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>` to `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onToggle'>`. This means the `onToggle` prop from `HTMLProps` is no longer accepted. Looking at the current usage of `FormFieldGroupExpandable` (lines 64-89), it doesn't use `onToggle`, so **no code change is needed**.

### Fix 3 (Line 68): Icon children to `icon` prop on Button
The `<Button>` on lines 68-77 contains only `<TrashIcon />` as a child. Per the fix instructions, this should be moved to the `icon` prop and the Button made self-closing.

Before:
```jsx
<Button
  aria-label={t('Remove peer')}
  className="co-create-networkpolicy__remove-peer"
  data-test="remove-peer"
  onClick={() => removePeer(index)}
  type="button"
  variant="plain"
>
  <TrashIcon />
</Button>
```

After:
```jsx
<Button
  aria-label={t('Remove peer')}
  className="co-create-networkpolicy__remove-peer"
  data-test="remove-peer"
  icon={<TrashIcon />}
  onClick={() => removePeer(index)}
  type="button"
  variant="plain"
/>
```

arguments.after:
<Button
                aria-label={t('Remove peer')}
                className="co-create-networkpolicy__remove-peer"
                data-test="remove-peer"
                icon={<TrashIcon />}
                onClick={() => removePeer(index)}
                type="button"
                variant="plain"
              />
arguments.before:
<Button
                aria-label={t('Remove peer')}
                className="co-create-networkpolicy__remove-peer"
                data-test="remove-peer"
                onClick={() => removePeer(index)}
                type="button"
                variant="plain"
              >
                <TrashIcon />
              </Button>

content.text: Let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. All imports are still used (`TrashIcon` is now referenced in the `icon` prop on line 72, `Button`, `Divider`, `FormFieldGroupExpandable`, `FormFieldGroupHeader` are all still used). No dead imports or artifacts.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 (Line 74) | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"plain"` is still valid in the new type. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'` to the union — no existing code is affected. |
| Fix 2 (Line 6) | `FormFieldGroupExpandable` signature changed | **No change needed** | The signature change removes `onToggle` from the accepted HTML props (`Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onToggle'>`). This file's usage of `FormFieldGroupExpandable` does not pass `onToggle`, so no code change is required. |
| Fix 3 (Line 68) | Icon children to `icon` prop on `Button` | **Applied** | Moved `<TrashIcon />` from Button children to the `icon` prop and made the Button self-closing. The Button now uses `icon={<TrashIcon />}` instead of wrapping `<TrashIcon />` as children content. |
